chunk_summary: Guidelines for inpatient management of SAM focus on triggers for reassessment, including hypoglycemia, hypothermia, persistent anorexia, oedema, and insufficient weight gain, especially when lab resources are unavailable.
learning_objectives: Identify clinical triggers for reassessment in SAM patients during inpatient stabilization.
Understand the importance of monitoring weight gain and oedema reduction in SAM management.
Learn resource-adapted approaches for hidden infection detection in SAM cases.
key_facts: Recurrent hypoglycemia or hypothermia are critical reassessment triggers in SAM management.
Persistent anorexia beyond expected recovery time requires full re-examination and referral.
Oedema not reducing or weight gain <5 g/kg/day after catch-up initiation mandates reassessment.
Resource-adapted guidelines prioritize clinical signs over lab investigations when labs are unavailable.
SAM management guidelines are supported by WHO and National Health Mission references.
reference_quote: If labs are unavailable, treat patterns such as recurrent hypoglycemia or hypothermia, persistent anorexia, oedema not reducing, or weight gain <5 g/kg/day as mandatory triggers for full re-examination and referral.
